skill.name: longbridge-fundamentals
skill.description: Analyze listed-company fundamentals with Longbridge data, including financial statements, valuation multiples, peer comparison, dividends, corporate actions, DCF models, and value screens.
skill.summary: Use Longbridge data to analyze financial statements, valuation, peer context, dividends, corporate actions, and DCF assumptions.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many shell, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and environment-access patterns, but review shows they are documentation false positives or legitimate Longbridge CLI instructions. The skill is prompt-only markdown and I found no prompt injection, credential exfiltration, destructive command, or malicious intent. Publication is reasonable with an external-command risk label because users may run Longbridge CLI commands.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown code spans flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are markdown code spans and fenced command examples. They document Longbridge CLI usage and do not execute inside the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown documentation and bash examples, not Ruby code or executable scripts. No file contains a script wrapper that would run these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptography patterns in finance text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in financial terminology, headings, and template text. I found no hash, digest, encryption, signing, or password handling code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe company profiles, valuation ranks, and premium or discount calculations. No cryptographic operation or weak algorithm use is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Environment and reconnaissance terms are not system access
low_findings.description: The environment-access and reconnaissance-style hits do not show credential reads, host enumeration, or system probing. One static env_access location is ordinary output guidance, and other similar text is CLI help documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss trading implications, valuation buckets, and peer-rank output. They do not read environment variables or gather host/system information.

low_findings.title: Legitimate external command dependency
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to call the Longbridge CLI and to use MCP as fallback. This is expected for the skill, but users should understand that analysis depends on an installed external tool and public market-data retrieval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CLI dependency is clearly documented and aligned with the skill purpose. Risk remains low because no destructive commands, shell metacharacter construction, or secret handling is shown.

content.value_statement: Investors need consistent financial data, valuation context, and peer comparisons before making research decisions. This skill uses Longbridge data to produce structured fundamental analysis, DCF assumptions, and screening results.
actual_capabilities: Fetch and summarize income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements for listed companies.
Compare PE, PB, PS, dividend yield, and valuation percentiles against industry peers.
Review dividends, business segments, corporate actions, company profiles, and executive data.
Build DCF analysis from free cash flow, WACC, terminal value, and margin of safety assumptions.
Screen index constituents for value or small-cap growth candidates using documented criteria.
Respond in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English based on the user request.
limitations: The skill depends on the Longbridge CLI or MCP being available in the user environment.
Some commands and flags may vary by Longbridge CLI version and require help lookup.
Sparse history or small peer groups can reduce percentile and industry comparison quality.
Outputs are research references only and do not constitute investment advice.

use_cases.title: Review a company before deeper research
use_cases.description: Get financial statements, valuation ratios, company profile details, and recent corporate actions in one structured answer.

use_cases.title: Build valuation ranges for a stock
use_cases.description: Use DCF, peer multiples, industry medians, and historical percentiles to frame an intrinsic value discussion.

use_cases.title: Screen markets for candidates
use_cases.description: Rank index constituents by PE, PB, ROE, dividend yield, revenue growth, and other documented factors.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize one company
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the latest financial statements, valuation metrics, dividend history, and company profile for AAPL.US.

prompt_templates.title: Compare valuation with peers
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare 700.HK with its industry peers using PE, PB, PS, and dividend yield. Explain any premium or discount.

prompt_templates.title: Estimate DCF value
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a base, bull, and bear DCF for NVDA.US using recent free cash flow, WACC assumptions, and terminal growth sensitivity.

prompt_templates.title: Run a value screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen the CSI 300 for stocks with PE below 20, PB below 2, ROE above 10%, and reasonable dividend yield.

output: Company overview with headquarters, listing details, and business description.
Financial statement summary covering revenue, net income, cash flow, and balance sheet strength.
Valuation section with PE, PB, PS, dividend yield, peer median, and historical context.
Clear caveats for missing fields and a not-investment-advice disclaimer.

output_examples.input: Is 700.HK expensive compared with its industry?
output: Peer valuation table with target company and industry median metrics.
Premium or discount calculation for PE, PB, and PS.
Percentile bucket explanation with notes on sample size and currency effects.
Balanced conclusion using Longbridge as the cited data source.

output_examples.input: Find value candidates in the S&P 500.
output: Screening parameters for market, index, PE, PB, ROE, and dividend yield.
Ranked shortlist with each company symbol, key metrics, and rationale.
Risk notes for cyclical sectors, sparse data, and valuation traps.

best_practices: Confirm the stock symbol uses Longbridge market format before running analysis.
Use multiple valuation views together instead of relying on one ratio.
Cite Longbridge Securities and include a research-only disclaimer in final output.
anti_patterns: Do not present screening or valuation output as a buy or sell instruction.
Do not invent values when Longbridge returns missing or sparse data.
Do not compare cross-currency market-cap metrics without noting currency effects.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It supports research and analysis only. It does not submit orders or automate trading.

faq.question: Which markets does it cover?
faq.answer: It is designed for Hong Kong, US, A-share, and Singapore listed companies supported by Longbridge.

faq.question: Does it require a Longbridge account?
faq.answer: The skill describes public data commands. Some local setups may still require Longbridge CLI configuration.

faq.question: Can it build a DCF model?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides free cash flow, WACC, terminal value, intrinsic value, and sensitivity analysis.

faq.question: How does it handle missing data?
faq.answer: It should state that data is unavailable, skip unsupported sections, and avoid unsupported conclusions.

faq.question: Can it answer in Chinese?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English responses.
